modules.RichDescription: ServiceNow can manage project tasks, readiness checklists, and release schedules, but it does not perform product build activities—often used for workflow coordination.
Supports quality assurance processes, defect tracking, and compliance workflows—especially in software QA—but not specialized manufacturing quality control.
Can manage R&D project tracking, resource allocation, and idea intake, but no native CAD, simulation, or lab data tools.

modules.ShortDescription: Agile module used today for sprint and stories
modules.RichDescription: Strong support via project portfolio management, Agile, and ITBM modules—manage, prioritize, and track requirements for software and product development.
ServiceNow can track engineering tasks and changes (especially for software engineering) but lacks mechanical/electrical design capabilities.
Can track campaigns, coordinate UAT/beta testing feedback, but not a specialized marketing analytics/testing platform
ServiceNow supports defining, managing, and enforcing development processes, especially for IT/software (DevOps, Agile, ITIL workflows).
Strong in software release orchestration, change management, and deployment tracking—especially for IT products. Less applicable to physical goods without integration.

Strong support for engineering change requests, approval workflows, and tracking—especially for software/configuration changes; manufacturing-specific change control needs integration.
ServiceNow excels in IT infrastructure management; for plant facilities or OT infrastructure, it can track assets, maintenance schedules, and incidents, but not control physical systems.
Can register, track, and monitor machine assets with CMDB and IoT integrations; lacks native control or real-time telemetry without external systems.
Asset & Facility Maintenance can be managed with Field Service Management and Asset Management, including preventive maintenance scheduling.
Can handle requests, tracking, and approvals for materials but not physical warehouse/inventory optimization—usually needs ERP/WMS integration.
Supports project and portfolio planning workflows, but no native CAD, CAM, or production line design capabilities.
Can track orders, status, and approvals via workflow, but actual production scheduling and execution require MES/ERP integration.
Can track KPIs, issues, and continuous improvement projects, but not real-time production control.
ServiceNow is well-suited for managing the lifecycle, deployment, and updates of manufacturing-related software systems.

modules.RichDescription: Global Compliance: Supports local HR compliance globally (multi-language, localizations).
Case Management: Tracks HR service requests and inquiries for faster resolution.
Knowledge Base: Empowers employees to self-resolve frequently asked questions.
Employee Centralization: Manage all employee details, jobs, positions, and organizational structures in one location.
Workforce Directory: Comprehensive employee directory with search capabilities.
Self-Service: Empowers employees and managers to update personal or job data.
Performance Management: Goal setting, periodic reviews, and feedback forms support employee development.
Succession Planning: Identifies high-potential employees and prepares them for leadership.
Compensation Cycle Management: Automates salary reviews, bonus, and incentive planning.
Budgeting: Allocates and manages budgets for compensation programs.
Pay Equity Analytics: Provides insights to drive equitable compensation practices.
Automated Payroll: Processes payroll for various employee types and locations.
Legal Compliance: Adapts to changing tax laws and regulations.
Integration: Connects with time, HR, and finance modules for accurate payout.
Shift Rosters: Easily manage complex shift patterns across departments.
Absence Management: Track and approve leaves or absences online.
Compliance: Ensures legal work-hour compliance and audits.
Eligibility Management: Determines employee eligibility for different benefits.
Enrollment and Life Events: Allows employees to enroll and update benefits based on life changes.
Reporting: Generates compliance and participation reports.
Personalized Learning Paths: Assigns courses based on job role and career goals.
Course Management: HR and managers can create, assign, and track completed courses.
Certification Tracking: Monitors expiry and renewal for required certifications.
Recruitment: Streamlines job posting, applicant tracking, and candidate selection with built-in analytics.
Phase out to Oracle
Phase out only in this context
Phase out to Lumina
Will not be phased out
